fact: August 16 sits exactly 228 days into the year, which means there are only 137 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for meteor showers - the Perseid meteor shower peaks right around now, shooting up to 60 streaks of light across the sky every hour!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest right now! These bug musicians can make sounds up to 120 decibels - that's louder than a chainsaw!
Why do sunflowers look so droopy in mid-August? They've finished following the sun and now permanently face east, waiting for their seeds to ripen!
August 16 falls smack in the middle of Leo season - people born today are ruled by the Sun itself, making them natural show-offs!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of winter, when some areas get their coldest temperatures of the year.
Hummingbirds are getting ready to migrate south and can gain 40% of their body weight in fat - imagine if humans did that before a long trip!
The moon on August 16 can appear extra orange or red when it rises, thanks to all the summer dust and humidity in the air acting like a giant filter.
Tree leaves start their color-changing process right around now, even though you can't see it yet - they're secretly breaking down their green chlorophyll!
In northern regions, the sun sets about 2-3 minutes earlier each day around August 16, and sunrise comes 1-2 minutes later - summer is sneaking away!
Spiders are at their biggest and boldest right now because they've been eating bugs all summer long - some orb weavers are as big as your palm!
August 16 sits exactly 33 days before the autumn equinox, when day and night become perfectly equal lengths everywhere on Earth.
